question.content: For the fixed-fixed beam AC with an internal couple at B (x_B=2L/3), identify the target deflection-function notation requested in the solution. Output exactly one expression token sequence in plain ASCII only, no Chinese characters, no unit text, no sentence, no equality sign, no chained equalities, and no extra text. Answer format: a single algebraic expression for the target function. Use the variable names from the problem and ASCII characters only; do not include units, explanatory text, or an equals sign.

step_1.content: Set fixed-fixed beam AC with internal couple at B where x_B=2L/3, then define piecewise coordinate on AB and BC.
step_1.key_point: Split at B and keep continuity of v and theta.

step_3.content: Build piecewise bending moments M_1(x), M_2(x), then use EI*v''(x)=M(x) in each segment.
step_3.key_point: Apply endpoint fixed conditions and B-point continuity.

step_5.content: Integrate along the analysis chain V -> M -> theta -> v and solve constants from boundary/continuity equations.

step_7.content: Assemble the full deflection curve over 0<=x<=L with segment matching at x=2L/3.
step_7.key_point: Curve is continuous with fixed-end constraints at A and C.
